evals.prompt: Check the quality of my Marp presentation slides.md
evals.expected_output: Runs slidegauge via uvx with --text flag, reports overall score, passing rate, and identifies failing slides with specific issues

assertions.text: Ran the slidegauge CLI tool (not just manual review)
Reported an overall/average score for the presentation
Identified specific slides that are failing (score < 70)
Named specific rule violations (e.g. bullets/too_many, title/too_long)

evals.prompt: My presentation has some slides scoring below 70, can you fix them?
evals.expected_output: Runs slidegauge with --json to get detailed diagnostics, identifies slides below threshold, applies appropriate fixes (splitting, condensing, shortening titles), then re-runs to validate improvements

assertions.text: Ran slidegauge analysis before making changes
Re-ran slidegauge after making fixes to verify improvement
All slides in the fixed version score >= 70
Did not remove or significantly alter the technical content

evals.prompt: Analyze my-talk.md with SlideGauge and fix any slides that have too many bullets
evals.expected_output: Runs slidegauge analysis, filters for bullets/too_many issues, splits or consolidates affected slides using Edit tool, re-validates with slidegauge

assertions.text: Ran slidegauge to identify bullet issues
Reduced bullet count to <= 6 per slide
Used split or consolidate strategy rather than deleting content
Re-ran slidegauge after fixes to confirm resolution
Focused on bullet issues, did not make unrelated changes
